Trust & Security

Zero OT network access. Zero inbound connections.

SAM4 is ISO 27001 certified, SOC 2 Type II audited, and built for environments where the security team says no to everything.

Certified and audited

Third-party validation, not promises. Our security posture is independently verified every year.

ISO 27001
Information Security Management
Certified ISMS covering data handling, access control, incident response, and supplier management.
Last renewed: January 2026
ISO 9001
Quality Management
Systematic quality management across hardware production, software development, and service delivery.
Last renewed: January 2026
SOC 2 Type II
Trust Services Controls
Independent audit of security, availability, and confidentiality controls across SAM4 infrastructure.

Data architecture

What SAM4 collects, where it lives, and how it gets there.

What we collect
Electrical current and voltage waveforms. No process data, no PLC data, no control system access.
Where it's stored
AWS EU data centres (Ireland). GDPR-compliant processing. All data owned by you, exportable on request.
How it gets there
TLS 1.2 encrypted outbound connections only. ~2 GB per gateway per month. AES-256 at rest.
SAM4 Edge
OAuth 2.0 + JWT
→
TLS 1.2
Outbound only
→
SAM4 Cloud
EU AWS
→
Dashboard
HTTPS

Outbound-only architecture: The Edge device initiates all connections. No inbound firewall rules needed. No open ports on your OT network. No remote access to plant systems.

Network and OT security

SAM4 operates alongside your OT network without touching it. No PLC connections, no control system integration, no inbound attack surface.

Network isolation
Edge devices sit on a separate VLAN. No bridging between IT, OT, and monitoring traffic. No sensors on assets.
Encrypted at every layer
TLS 1.2 in transit. AES-256 at rest. OAuth 2.0 device authentication. No shared secrets.
No inbound connections
Edge initiates all communication. Firewall allows outbound HTTPS only. No listening services on your network.
Passive measurement only
Current transformers read without interrupting circuits. SAM4 never controls plant equipment. Read-only by physics.

Security controls overview

How SAM4 maps to enterprise security requirements.

Domain Control Standard Last reviewed
Identity & Access Role-based access, least privilege, regular access reviews ISO 27001 January 2026
Encryption TLS 1.2 in transit, AES-256 at rest ISO 27001 January 2026
Network Separate VLAN, outbound-only connections, no inbound ports ISO 27001 January 2026
Infrastructure Infrastructure as code (Terraform), compliance scanning, no admin services on internet ISO 27001 January 2026
Application Code reviews, automated builds, threat modelling, vulnerability scanning ISO 27001 January 2026
Monitoring & Logging VPC flow logs, event log analysis, 24/7 infrastructure monitoring SOC 2 Type II January 2026
Personnel Background checks, NDA clauses, managed endpoints, access reviews ISO 27001 January 2026
Penetration Testing Annual pen tests by Secura. March 2025. No outstanding vulnerabilities. ISO 27001 March 2025

Cloud platform security

SAM4 runs on AWS in EU data centres, with defence-in-depth at every layer.

EU data residency
All data stored and processed in EU AWS data centres. GDPR-compliant data handling throughout.
Role-based access
Granular RBAC with SSO integration. Multi-factor authentication enforced. Session timeouts and audit logging.
Continuous monitoring
24/7 infrastructure monitoring. Automated vulnerability scanning. Annual pen testing by Secura. No outstanding vulnerabilities.

Regulatory compliance

SAM4 supports compliance with EU cybersecurity and data protection requirements.

NIS2 Directive
Outbound-only architecture, network isolation, and incident response procedures align with NIS2 requirements for essential and important entities in energy, water, and manufacturing.
GDPR
All customer data processed and stored in EU AWS data centres (Ireland). Data processing agreements available. Customer data is owned by you and can be exported or deleted on request.
Industry standards
Security controls map to IEC 62443 principles for industrial automation security. Penetration testing conducted annually by Secura.

IT/OT gatekeeper checklist

The questions your security team will ask, and the answers they need to approve SAM4.

  • Does it touch the control network? No. Separate VLAN, outbound-only.
  • Can it control plant equipment? No. Passive current measurement only.
  • Where is data stored? EU AWS data centres (Ireland).
  • Is data encrypted? TLS 1.2 in transit, AES-256 at rest.
  • Does it require open firewall ports? No. Outbound HTTPS (443) only.
  • Is it independently audited? ISO 27001 and SOC 2 Type II certified.
  • Can we restrict user access? Yes. RBAC with SSO and MFA.
  • What data does it collect? Electrical current and voltage waveforms only.

Security FAQ

No. SAM4 measures electrical current through passive current transformers. It has no physical or logical connection to control systems, PLCs, or SCADA networks. The measurement is passive and read-only by design.
The SAM4 Edge device buffers data locally and resumes upload when connectivity returns. No data is lost. The asset continues running normally. SAM4 never affects plant operations.
SAM4 is a cloud-native platform. On-premise deployments are available for enterprise customers with specific regulatory requirements. Contact our team to discuss options.
Our ISO 27001 ISMS includes a documented incident response procedure. Customers are notified within 24 hours of any confirmed security incident affecting their data. We conduct post-incident reviews and publish findings to affected customers.
Yes. We provide ISO 27001 certificates, SOC 2 reports, data processing agreements, and a detailed security whitepaper to customers and prospects under NDA. Contact our team to request access.

Request security documentation

ISO 27001 certificate, SOC 2 report, data processing agreement, and security whitepaper. Everything your IT team needs to approve SAM4.

Request Security Docs

Monitoring the assets your sensors can't reach.
